description: A vertical, portrait-orientation warning graphic from USCIS. The top half is a color photograph of fraudulent identity documents spread on a white surface: a dark blue passport (lettered 'PASSPORT'), an open Australian passport photo page with a man's photo and machine-readable 'AUS<<<' code, an Australian ID/driver-license card with a gold EMV chip, several loose passport-style headshot photos of a bearded man, and an open pair of metal scissors lying across the documents (implying document forgery/cutting). The bottom half is a solid dark navy panel containing the official DHS Department of Homeland Security circular seal (eagle crest) above 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' in grey serif type. Below, the headline in bold yellow/cream serif reads 'Providing a fake document to USCIS is fraud,' and beneath it in bold white sans-serif: 'Fraud can get you permanently banned from the U.S.' The design uses an evidence-photo plus official-seal authority aesthetic. The messaging technique is deterrence/warning, threatening permanent immigration consequences for document fraud. Target audience is immigrants and applicants, warning against fake green cards and identity documents.
notable_text: Providing a fake document to USCIS is fraud; Fraud can get you permanently banned from the U.S.
